description: Define a dedicated Temporal workflow/activity pair for proactive NinjaOne token refresh
Compute a proactive refresh target time from stored NinjaOne credential expiry using a configurable safety buffer
Schedule one delayed future refresh workflow per active NinjaOne integration when credentials are first connected
Reschedule the next future refresh workflow after each successful proactive token refresh
Reschedule the proactive refresh workflow after successful lazy refreshes so background ownership stays current
Reload the latest stored NinjaOne credentials at workflow execution time instead of trusting stale schedule input
Use the existing NinjaOne OAuth refresh-token contract to rotate access token, refresh token, and expiry during proactive refresh
Persist rotated NinjaOne credentials back to tenant secret storage after successful proactive refresh
Persist integration-owned lifecycle metadata for NinjaOne token refresh schedule and reconnect-required state without storing raw tokens
Ensure only one future proactive refresh execution is active per NinjaOne integration at a time
Mark the integration as reconnect-required on terminal provider/token failures such as invalid refresh token
Stop or invalidate future proactive scheduling after terminal reconnect-required failures until the integration is reconnected or reset
Keep existing lazy NinjaOne token refresh behavior as a fallback for missed schedules or unscheduled integrations
Seed proactive refresh scheduling for already-active NinjaOne integrations during rollout using their stored credential expiry
Treat integrations with missing or unreadable NinjaOne credentials as unschedulable and record an explicit failure state instead of silently looping
Disconnecting NinjaOne cancels or safely no-ops any future proactive refresh execution for that integration
Reconnecting NinjaOne replaces stale lifecycle metadata and seeds a fresh future proactive refresh schedule
Emit structured logs for schedule creation, execution start, success, retryable failure, and terminal reconnect-required failure
Reuse or extend existing integration token lifecycle event publication so proactive NinjaOne refresh failures remain visible outside worker logs
Scope workflow ownership to the existing app Temporal worker/task queue used by NinjaOne sync execution unless explicit queue separation is required
